source_metadata.title_raw: Rialto Unified School District - A-18-218 - December 21, 2018 - Rialto

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

December 21, 2018

Rialto Unified School District
Dina Walker, District Board Member
Cuauhtemoc Avila, Ed.D
Superintendent
182 E. Walnut Avenue
Rialto, CA 92376

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-218

Dear Ms. Walker and Mr. Avila:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ and Government Code section 1090. Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Bernardino County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Do the conflict of interest provisions of either Section 1090 or the Act prohibit District Board Member Dina Walker, the President and CEO of BLU Educational Foundation ("BLU"), from participating in the District Board's approval of a data sharing agreement between the District and BLU?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Under Section 1090, Board Member Walker has a financial interest in the data sharing agreement and may not participate in the District Board's approval of the contract. However, because her interest in the contract is remote, the District may nonetheless enter the contract with BLU so long as she follows the abstention requirements set forth below.

_______________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-218
Page No. 2

FACTS

This request is made jointly by the Rialto Unified School District ("District") and Board Member Walker, who is the President and CEO of BLU, a 501(c)(3) non-profit corporation. Board Member Walker receives income for her service as BLU's President and CEO. As stated on its website, BLU's mission is "[t]o provide educational and human services programming to youth, adults and organizations in order to build healthy, productive communities."

BLU received a 2018 award from Growing Inland Achievement ("GIA"), which is "a collective impact collaborative focused on improving the long-term economic outlook of the region through improving the educational attainment rates of the Inland Empire."2 BLU was one of six organizations to earn a 2018 GIA Innovation Award, and its winning proposal was the College Success Initiative designed to increase college access and completion among traditionally disadvantaged students of color.

Pursuant to the College Success Initiative, BLU will train and oversee members of the College Success Corps, which is made up of college students who provide hands-on assistance (group-based and one-on-one) to District students and parents, focused on college attendance and completing college admission, financial aid and scholarship applications. College Success Corps staff will advise District students and parents both during the school day and after school hours.

As part of the College Success Initiative program, BLU seeks to enter into a data sharing agreement with the District in order to track and share data to document student outcomes and the College Success Initiative's progress. For example, BLU would gather data to compare the outcomes of the College Success Initiative participants to nonparticipants. This data sharing agreement would not have a financial component, but would require District Board approval.3

ANALYSIS

Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, from making contracts in which they are financially interested. Section 1090 is concerned with financial interests, other than remote or minimal interests, that prevent public officials from exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests of their agencies. (Sigall v. Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) Section 1090 is intended "not only to strike at actual impropriety, but also to strike at the appearance of impropriety." (City of Imperial Beach v. Bailey (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 191, 197.) A contract that violates Section 1090 is void. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.) The prohibition applies regardless of whether the terms of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Id. at pp. 646-649.)

_____________

2 (See http://inlandempiregia.org.)

3 By email dated November 19, 2018, Board Member Walker indicated that it is not necessary for the Board to approve the District's participation in the College Success Initiative program. As such, the data sharing agreement is the only component of the program that the Board will need to approve.

File No. A-18-218
Page No. 3

You have asked whether Section 1090 prohibits Board Member Walker from participating
in the District Board's approval of a data sharing agreement between the District and BLU. The
determinative question is whether Board Member Walker has a financial interest in such an
agreement.

Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has
a financial interest" (People v. Honig (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333), and officials are deemed to
have a financial interest in a contract if they might profit from it in any way. (Ibid.) Although
Section 1090 nowhere specifically defines the term "financial interest," case law and Attorney
General Opinions state that prohibited financial interests may be indirect as well as direct, and may
involve financial losses, or the possibility of losses, as well as the prospect of pecuniary gain. (See
e.g., Thomson, supra, at pp. 645, 651-652.)

In addition, case law and statutory exceptions to Section 1090 make clear that the term
"financially interested" must be liberally interpreted. (See, e.g., People v. Deysher (1934) 2 Cal.2d
141, 146 ["(h)owever devious and winding the chain may be which connects the officer with the
forbidden contract, if it can be followed and the connection made, the contract is void"].) Further,
"the certainty of financial gain is not necessary to create a conflict of interest... (t)he government's
right to the absolute, undivided allegiance of a public officer is diminished as effectively where the
officer acts with a hope of personal financial gain as where he acts with certainty." (People v. Gnass
(2002) 101 Cal.App.4th 1271, 1298 (citations omitted).)

By entering into a data sharing agreement with the District, BLU will gain access to student
data that it would not otherwise have. It will thus be able to track and share this data to document
student outcomes and the College Success Initiative's progress. For example, the data will allow
BLU to compare outcomes of program participants to those of nonparticipants. Possessing this type
of information undoubtedly has some value to BLU, especially if it seeks to secure similar future
grants. Therefore, as President and CEO of BLU, Board Member Walker has a financial interest in
the data sharing agreement between the District and BLU.

The Legislature, however, has created various statutory exceptions to Section 1090's
prohibition where the financial interest involved is deemed a "remote interest," as defined in
Section 1091, or a "noninterest," as defined in Section 1091.5. If a "remote interest" is present, the
contract may be made if: (1) the officer discloses his or her financial interest in the contract to the
public agency; (2) the interest is noted in the public agency's official records; and (3) the officer
abstains from any participation in the making of the contract. (Section 1091(a); 88
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 106, 108 (2005).) If a "noninterest" is present, the contract may be made without
the officer's abstention. (City of Vernon v. Central Basin Mun. Water Dist. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th
508, 514-515.)

Section 1091(b)(1) sets forth a remote interest exception and provides in pertinent part as
follows:

(b) As used in this article, "remote interest" means any of the
following:

File No. A-18-218
Page No. 4

(1) That of an officer or employee of a nonprofit entity exempt
from taxation pursuant to Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue
Code (26 U.S.C. Sec. 501(c)(3)), pursuant to Section 501(c)(5) of the
Internal Revenue Code (26 U.S.C. Sec. 501(c)(5)), or a nonprofit
corporation.

As the President and CEO of BLU, a 501(c)(3) non-profit corporation, Board Member
Walker would have only a remote interest in any data sharing agreement between the District and
BLU pursuant to Section 1091(b)(1). Because she has a remote interest in the potential data sharing
agreement, the District may enter into that agreement only if: (1) she discloses her financial interest
in the agreement to the District; (2) her interest is noted in the District's official records; and (3) she
abstains from any participation in the making of the agreement. (Section 1091(a); 88
Ops.CalAtty.Gen. 106, 108 (2005)).⁴

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By:
Jack Woodside
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

JW:jgl

_______________________
⁴ By following the abstention requirements of Section 1090 and additionally leaving the room during the
decision, Board Member Walker will satisfy the Act's recusal provisions set forth in Section 87105 and Regulation
18707. Therefore, we do not provide further analysis under the Act's conflict of interest provisions.

from making contracts in which they are financially interested. Section 1090 is concerned with

contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Id. at pp. 646-649.)
